capacity_evidence.reason: The optimized-decoder stage sets the optimized-decoder validated prefill context to 128 tokens and adds paged decode coverage. The default paged KV cache can hold 128 logical tokens. The cache dtype changed from BF16 to BF8_B after real-weight cache/fidelity timing evidence; logical capacity is unchanged. Full HF-context dense prefill is not feasible for this decoder implementation because setup currently materializes a dense causal mask of shape [1, 1, max_seq_len, max_seq_len]; at 131072 positions this is 17,179,869,184 float32 elements, about 64 GiB before TT padding, cache, activations, or weights. The 128-token contract is the largest validated optimized-decoder context in this stage, not a model-wide serving limit.
capacity_evidence.next_stage_expectation: Later full-model stages may extend validated context with full stack evidence; this optimized decoder stage does not reduce advertised capability.

evidence.command: pytest -q -s models/autoports/meta_llama_llama_3_1_8b_instruct/tests/test_optimized_decoder.py --tb=short plus focused seq128 probe recorded in doc/optimized_decoder/work_log.md

notes: HF-advertised context 131072 is taken from the repo-local Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct config mirror under models/tt_transformers/model_params and matches the forge emit shapes.

Current supported context records the largest optimized decoder prefill sequence validated in this stage; full HF-context dense prefill is blocked by the quadratic causal-mask allocation described in capacity_evidence.
Optimized decode uses TTNN paged KV cache update and paged_scaled_dot_product_attention_decode with non-aligned prefix coverage.
Paged KV cache tensors use shape [max_num_blocks, num_kv_heads, block_size, head_dim] with BF8_B cache dtype by default.
